verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 is a tetrameric, six-TM-helix IP3-gated Ca2+ release channel whose TM stalk is embedded in the ER membrane and whose entire large ligand-binding/regulatory cap is exposed to the cytosol — not to the extracellular space. All five contextual buckets were checked: (1) cell_state_induced — no gene-specific evidence of outer-leaflet display in cancer, apoptosis, or activation states; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no documented PM display in any tissue; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — ITPR1 is not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM minority pool by surface biotinylation, flow on intact cells, or surface proteomics; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to PM. Clinical autoantibodies (anti-Sj/ITPR1-IgG) target an intracellular epitope detectable only in permeabilized tissue or transfected-cell assays, not an extracellular surface pool. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic program targets the protein body from outside the cell.
